title: Trace Lookup (trace-lookup v1.0)
description: Request/response contract for POST /api/trace_lookup. Typed read-only facade over stored query_trace artifacts. Only artifacts of type 'query_trace' are returned; all other artifact types yield status 'not_found'.

id.description: The artifact ID returned at query time (e.g. 'qart-<hex>'). Must refer to a stored query_trace artifact.

status.description: Lookup outcome. 'ok': trace found. 'not_found': no artifact with this id, or artifact exists but is not query_trace. 'error': internal failure (e.g. store not initialized).

id.description: The artifact ID that was requested.

trace.description: The query_trace payload. Null when status is not 'ok'.

provenance.description: Provenance metadata (source_query, timestamp, index_id, run_id). Null when status is not 'ok'.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this artifact was stored. Null when status is not 'ok'.

warnings.description: Non-fatal diagnostic messages (e.g. type mismatch naming the actual artifact type, store not initialized).

authority.description: Authority class of this runtime artifact. Always 'runtime_observation' for query_trace artifacts.

canonicality.description: Canonicality class. Always 'observation' for query_trace artifacts.

artifact_shape.description: Shape of the stored trace data. Always 'raw' for query_trace: unmodified internal execute_query output.

retention_policy.description: Retention policy for this artifact in the store. 'unbounded_currently' means no GC is applied.

lifecycle_status.description: Lifecycle status of this runtime artifact. 'active' means the artifact is present and not expired. No GC or TTL is applied currently.

expires_at.description: Expiry timestamp (ISO 8601) if a TTL is set; null while retention_policy is 'unbounded_currently'.

does_not_prove.description: Statements this artifact does not prove.

claim_boundaries.description: Epistemic boundaries of this runtime artifact.
